name: Noble Sissle
abstract: Noble Lee Sissle (July 10, 1889 – December 17, 1975) was an American jazz composer, lyricist, bandleader, singer, and playwright, best known for the Broadway musical Shuffle Along (1921), and its hit song "I'm Just Wild About Harry". He also served as the first president of the Negro Actors Guild.

concerts.label: Afternoon at Carnegie Hall to Benefit Music for the Blind

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I knew about the band. I knew about Eubie Blake and Noble Sissle and these people, although I wasn’t professionally playing any music.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: So he heard of me and called me at my hotel, and said, 'Come on. Would you like to go with me? I'm going up to British Columbia on a theater tour.' ... So four days I worked, and then finally I left to go with Noble Sissle, up in British Columbia, Seattle, Portland, San Francisco.

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur Briggs
about_quotes.evidence: I made my audition for Noble Sissle, and I got accepted... So, I worked with Sissle. We went backwards and forwards to Monte Carlo, and Paris...

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: Noble is uh somebody my father used to talk about: Noble Sissle, you know. Even though he'd never heard him, he just heard of him, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: I went to Noble Sissle -- late '31, I went to Noble -- '32, in '32, I think. ... So there I play ... with Noble Sissle

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I had seen Sissle on stage when I was in Cincinnati... I was never a lover of the soprano sax so Bechet didn't tell me anything... I felt sorry for the musicians because they had no leader that knew beans about jazz.
